title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: A.J. Brown trade to the Patriots may finalize after June 1 due to Eagles' salary cap considerations.
Sports Illustrated reports a trade framework is in place; Patriots favored to acquire Brown from Eagles.
Eagles seek a first-round pick, but Patriots are hesitant; NFL Network says talks are not close.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents across Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom on Saturday afternoon, leading to widespread social media speculation.
Meteorologists identified the noise as a sonic boom from a bolide meteor entering the atmosphere near the South Shore.
Officials confirmed no emergency incidents were related to the boom, dismissing theories of an earthquake or man-made explosion.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in his opener debut, leading to a 4-3 Red Sox loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in bulk relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox dropped to 23-33, ten games under .500, after losing six of their last seven games.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics fans and the TD Garden environment during a recent livestream.
Boston is seen as a contender for Antetokounmpo despite Miami Heat being the favorite to land him.
Speculation remains regarding a potential trade involving Jaylen Brown for Antetokounmpo this offseason.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police cleared the Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd concerns shortly after 5 a.m.
Massachusetts State Police were called as graduates blocked the bridge and attempted to swim in the prohibited Charles River.
The event followed the Class of 2026's commencement where Conan O'Brien delivered the keynote address.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now available on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman's enhanced performance.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2, starring Tina Fey and Steve Carell, streams on Netflix with improved storytelling elements.
'Spider-Noir' with Nicolas Cage as a 1930s private detective is streaming on Prime Video in color and black & white.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox predicted to draft third baseman Bo Lowrance, 18, by ESPN's Kiley McDaniel in 2026 MLB mock drafts.
Lowrance, from Christ Church Episcopal High School, is 6'5", 200 pounds, committed to the University of Virginia.
Scouts compare Lowrance to Freddie Freeman; he has a smooth left-handed stroke but lacks explosive bat speed.

title: Lowell father and daughter charged in alleged payroll tax scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lim Ou, 69, and Nalen Ou, 39, from Lowell, charged with conspiracy and failure to pay payroll taxes.
Prosecutors allege the Ous concealed over $48 million in cash payroll through unreported wages in their staffing firms.
The charges could lead to five years in prison, supervised release, and fines up to $250,000 if convicted.

title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Christian Gonzalez is unlikely to attend Patriots OTAs without a contract extension, reports NBC Sports Boston.
Gonzalez aims for a salary increase from $4 million to $32-35 million per year, aligning with top NFL corners.
The Patriots restructured a contract for cap space, possibly aiding Gonzalez's extension negotiations.

title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A suspect wanted for drug offenses led a police chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham on Friday.
The driver struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden officer before abandoning the car in Stoneham.
Police are searching for the driver who fled on foot with a passenger; no injuries were reported.
